name: Linda's Potato Skins
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Milk | Optional for mashed centers
1/4 cup Butter | Optional for mashed centers
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Season potatoes with salt and pepper if desired.
2. Scrub potatoes clean, dry them thoroughly, then pierce several times with a fork.
3. Arrange on a baking sheet and bake for about 1 hour or until tender. Let cool slightly before handling.
4. Cut each potato in half lengthwise and scoop out the center, leaving about 1/8 inch of potato in the shell.
5. Cover shells and refrigerate up to 2 days if not using them immediately.
6. If desired, mix 1/3 cup milk, 1/4 cup butter, salt, and pepper into the potato centers for mashed potatoes.
7. Preheat oven to 500°F (260°C). Melt 1/3 cup butter.
8. Brush both sides of the shells with the melted butter.
9. Place cut side up on a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes or until brown and crisp.
10. Distribute bacon, onion, and cheese in each shell. Bake just until the cheese melts.
11. Serve hot. If the potatoes are large, you may want to cut them crosswise before serving.
12. This recipe can be doubled if serving several people, especially hungry guests.
